title: botrychium australe subsp. negeri
intro: Botrychium australe subsp. negeri, also known as Neger's grapefern, is a rare and endangered species of fern found in the Mediterranean region. It belongs to the family Ophioglossaceae and is characterized by its unique, grape-like fronds. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the immune system and exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Traditionally, Neger's grapefern is prepared as a herbal tea or tincture to promote health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function.
